properties.remark: AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR OLD WADLEY ROAD AND HIGHWAY 17. THE STORM TRACKED NORTH-NORTHEAST 1.3 MILES WITH WIDTH OF 250 YARDS BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR BARK CAMP CHURCH ROAD. THE TORNADO DOWNED SEVERAL TREES AND TURNED OVER AN IRRIGATION SYSTEM. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 3 MINUTES.
PUBLIC REPORTED TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ACROSS HIGHWAY 17 NW OF MIDVILLE GA. WIND DAMAGE EXTENDS NORTH OF HIGHWAY 17 AS WELL. IRRIGATION SYSTEM FLIPPED AND DESTROYED.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED NEAR REEVES ROAD. THE STORM TRACKED NORTHEAST 1.5 MILES WITH A WIDTH OF 100 YARDS BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR ROSIER ROAD. THE TORNADO DAMAGED A METAL BARN ROOF AND SEVERAL DAIRY CALF WEANING HUTS. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 2 MINUTES.

TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN, MAINLY IN THE VICINITY OF REYNOLDS ROAD AND ASHLEIGH ROAD. HOWEVER, IT WAS INCONCLUSIVE THAT A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THIS AREA EARLIER TODAY AS IT WAS WITHIN THE EXACT PATH OF THE TORNADO WHICH MOVED THROUGH THIS SAME AREA ON JANUARY 21 2017. THERE WERE SOME PINE TREES BELIEVED TO HAVE BEEN DAMAGED TODAY THAT WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED, BUT THEY WERE ALL FACING TOWARD THE NORTHEAST OR EAST- NORTHEAST, LEADING TOWARD THE DETERMINATION THAT STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OCCURRED WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED TORNADO DAMAGE ON THE EAST SIDE OF JOHNSTON NEAR WINTHROW AVENUE AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST THROUGH WARD BEFORE DISSIPATING NORTHWEST OF RIDGE SPRING OFF OF MURPHY FARM ROAD. WIDESPREAD HARDWOOD AND PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED CAUSING DAMAGE TO MULTIPLE HOMES AND VEHICLES. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS EF-0 AND EF-1, BUT EF-2 DAMAGE WITH PEAK WIND SPEEDS NEAR 115 MPH WAS NOTED NEAR WARD. WIDESPREAD LARGE HARDWOODS WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED WITH SOME TREES HAVING THE BARK STRIPPED OFF. IN ADDITION, THE TOP OF A CONCRETE SILO WAS DESTROYED.
GRAIN SILO HEAVILY DAMAGED AND MULTIPLE TREES ALONG WITH POWER LINES DOWN AT HWY 39 AND MURPHY FARM ROAD.

TORNADO DAMAGE CONFIRMED NEAR HOLLY HILL, SC. THE EF1 TORNADO, WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 103 MPH, WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 3.1 MILES FROM WHITE SANDS ROAD INTO THE TOWN OF HOLLY HILL. THE TORNADO SNAPPED/UPROOTED MULTIPLE TREES, DESTROYED A MOBILE HOME, DAMAGED MULTIPLE VEHICLES AND PEELED BACK THE METAL ROOF OF A STRIP MALL.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTHWEST OLAR, SC AND DISSIPATED APPROXIMATELY 4 MINUTES LATER NEAR GOVAN, SC. THE TORNADO WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THE REMNANTS OF HURRICANE IRMA.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL. PUBLIC REPORT FROM NEIGHBORING OFFICE.

THE NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM FOUND AN EF1 TORNADO THAT BEGAN NEAR LAKE GREENWOOD NEAR PINELAND ROAD. THE TORNADO LIFTED AND TOUCHED DOWN IN MULTIPLE LOCATIONS AS IT MOVED NORTH-NORTHEAST. THE SECOND LOCATION WAS ALONG WORKMEN KELLY ROAD. THE THIRD LOCATION WAS ALONG HIGHWAY 39 NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SALTER ROAD AND THE LAST LOCATION WAS ALONG OLD CRAVEN PLACE LANE. THE TORNADO ENDED BEFORE IT REACHED VAUGHNVILLE CHURCH ROAD. THE PATH WAS 2.41 MILES LONG AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. THE DURATION OF THE TORNADO EVENT WAS 7 MINUTES.
